author: sebastianmc
description: Manual or automatic config-driven reordering and sorting of files and folders in File Explorer.

manifest.author: SebastianMC
manifest.description: Allows for manual and automatic, config-driven reordering and sorting of files and folders in File Explorer
manifest.name: Custom File Explorer sorting

name: Custom File Explorer sorting

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: #216 - bugfix for broken sorting by metadata when one or both metadata are boolean `false`

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
#211 - allow smaller values of initial delay, including zero
- also increased the precision to one decimal place (tenths of a second)
- for desktop-only vaults or local-only vaults or relatively small vaults the Obsidian needs less time to read the files and populate metadata cache. Users can adjust the initial delay below 1 second, including zero (no delay)
- the default initial value remains 1 second (new vaults, new plugin installs) as a tradeoff

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Bugfix of #205: Performance degradation over time due to recursive monkey-patching of FileExplorer

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
#202 Improve automatic initial application of custom sorting for Obsidian deferred views
* added monitoring of the deferred File Explorer state (w/o performance impact) to automatically apply custom sorting when it becomes a fully-fledged File Explorer view

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
1. Support for more formats of dates extraction from titles: `\[dd-mm-yyyy]` and `\[mm-dd-yyyy]`
2. A different error message on mobile when the File Explorer view is not available (e.g. is deferred).

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Date patterns in titles of files and folders - support for more patterns:
- #178 - week number patterns: `\[yyyy-Www]`, `\[yyyy-WwwISO]`, `\[yyyy-Www (mm-dd)]`. Finetuning with `W1-` or `W35+` to sort before or after first / last day of specified week
- #191 - plain dates `\[yyyy-mm-dd]` and `\[yyyy-dd-mm]`

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Fully reshaped logic of initial application of custom sorting for compatibility with Obsidian 1.7.2 and newer
- custom sorting is applied automatically after plugin load / start with configured delay (1 second by default)
- the delay is configurable in range from 1 - 30 seconds
- for large vaults, large number of plugins or on mobile the delay might need to be increased to allow successful automatic application of custom sort
- improved plugin state management and notifications
- some more improvements

Obsidian 1.7.2 or newer is required

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Dropped support for Obsidian earlier that 1.7.2
- #185
- Obsidian 1.7.2 or newer is required
- no functional changes - feature set is same as in the recent release [2.1.15](https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort/releases/tag/2.1.15)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Support for new date format in folder / file names
- #184: Sort notes in Mmm-dd-yyyy format

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Compatibility with Obsidian 1.7.2
- #160 Plugin broken in obsidian 1.7.2

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
#156 - improved support for index-note-based folder notes
- metadata of index-note-based folder notes are treated as parent folder metadata and can be used for sorting, if configured in the updated settings window
- backward compatible, no config changes required if no need to take advantage of the new feature

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
New syntax for matching dates in specific format: dd-Mmm-yyyy
- #151 - Sort notes in dd-Mmm-yyyy format
  - usage example in a [comment under 151](https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort/issues/151#issuecomment-2220112669)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Full compatibility with Obsidian 1.6.3
- #147 A race condition (first observed for Obsidian 1.6.3) could cause the plugin not to apply the custom sort automatically on vault opening / loading or app (re)loading

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Minor polishing: let the plugin be a good Obsidian citizen when it gets uninstalled or removed:
#137 - Impose File Explorer refresh with standard Obsidian sorting when the plugin gets disabled or uninstalled.

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Compatibility with Obsidian 1.6.0
- #145: 1.6 breaks plugin
- Obsidian 1.6.0 introduced some internal breaking changes. This release contains the necessary adjustments to the plugin implied by Obsidian 1.6.0

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
- #132 - (experimental) support for two new sorting orders: `advanced recursive modified` and `advanced recursive created`
  - as their names suggest, they are extensions of `advanced modified` and `advanced created`, respectively. Folder modified and created dates are determined by recursively scanning of all descendants
- removal of experimental not working sorting methods: `aaa-z` and `a-zzz`
- added alias `unicode-charcode` for the existing `vsc-unicode` order to be more descriptive for non VSC users

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Bugfix for #131 - Obsidian 1.5.4 (insider build) introduced breaking changes around File Explorer JS structure which prevented this custom-sort plugin from working.
- fix backward compatible with versions earlier that 1.5.4

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Two experimental features (they are subject to change or removal, exposed for testing and collecting feedback)

- `aaa-z` and `a-zzz` sort orders (_uppercase first, alphanumeric_ order and _lowercase  first, alphanumeric_)
- `vsc-unicode` sort order, which is equivalent to what VS Code refers to as 'unicode' sorting (which name is arbitrary and confusing, yet familar to VS Code users)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
#126 - Allow to eliminate the ambiguity of four dots `....`
- by default `....` means _three-dots (a wildcard) with trailing dot_
- use `./...` syntax to impose _leading dot followed by three-dots (the wildcard)_


#127 - support for scenarios when file basename and folder name are the same and their relative order is undetermined by other sorting criteria (e.g. by modified / created dates)
- by default the last-resort last-level implicit sorting gives priority to a file in the described case
- new syntax introduced `files-first` and `folders-first` to allow explicit controlling of the order, e.g. `  < a-z, files first`

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
#123 - sorting now uses basename of file (w/o extension) by default (fix of a retro-bug).
- New syntax `a-z.` and `true a-z.` introduced to allow explicitly sorting by filename with ext (new feature)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
#115 Add inline regex support to match capital letters and lowercase letters explicitly
- \C introduced to match **C**apital letters
- \l introduced to match **l**owercase letters

No functional changes vs 2.1.1 release, only removed console.log statements which slipped in

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
#115 Add inline regex support to match capital letters and lowercase letters explicitly
- \C introduced to match **C**apital letters
- \l introduced to match **l**owercase letters

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Changed the sorting behavior for items with undefined attributes:
- sorting by bookmarks, item w/o bookmark status pushed to the bottom in both: asc and desc orders
- sorting by metadata, item w/o relevant metadata pushed to the bottom in both, asc and desc orders
- sorting by creation or modification date, advanced mode: empty folders (or folders having only folder children) are pushed to the bottom, regardless of asc or desc order

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
New feature: support for drag and drop ordering of File Explorer items via Bookmarks integration
- detailed instructions available on [project wiki page: How to order items with drag and drop](https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort/wiki/How-to-order-items-in-File-Explorer-with-drag-and-drop%3F)
- feel free to post questions and report issues in the github home of the plugin: [Custom File Explorer Sorting](https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort)
- no functional changes vs 2.0.1 release, only updated project metadata for consistency

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
New feature: support for drag and drop ordering of File Explorer items via Bookmarks integration
- detailed instructions available on [project wiki page: How to order items with drag and drop](https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort/wiki/How-to-order-items-in-File-Explorer-with-drag-and-drop%3F)
- feel free to post questions and report issues in the github home of the plugin: [Custom File Explorer Sorting](https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Bugfix for broken integration with other plugins in 1.9.0. Now it works again.

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
- multi-level sorting support added, the syntax is to use comma to separate the 2nd level:
  - e.g.: `< a-z by-metadata: date, > a-z by-metadata: time`
- support to derive the order currently set in Obsidian UI and apply it to a subset of items in a folder
  - the syntax is: `sorting: standard` or `sorting: ui selected` (both are equivalent)
- more fine-grained messages for syntax errors in sorting order specification
  - instead of the generic message like:
    - ```7:InvalidAttributeValue Invalid value of the attribute ">"```
 - you will see now:
    - ```7:InvalidAttributeValue Secondary sorting order contains unrecognized text: >>> sorting <<<```, or
    - ```7:InvalidAttributeValue Secondary sorting order contains unrecognized text: >>> : <<<```,
    - or similar
- refactoring for more readable and more concise code
- bug fixes

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
New feature: basic macros support
- initially only _parent folder name_ placeholder, useful in wildcard-based `target-folder:` scenarios, details in comments under #79
- from user perspective it can be used to consciously position the _folder notes_ in folders (if _folder notes_ are not hidden)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Bugfix: #72 `< advanced created` sorting method didn't work correctly

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Added support for two new sorting / grouping symbols (#67):
- `\a+` to match any ASCII word
- `\A+` to match any modern language word

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
New feature: integration with `obsidian-icon-folder` plugin:
  - you can now group folders and files based on the presence and/or name of icon configured using that plugin
  - More details in #60

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Bugfix: #58 - Some `target-folder:` get ignored when sorting specs are read from two or more notes (bug was introduced in 1.6.0)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Minor: mobile-specific tweaks, e.g. new ribbon icon and documentation updates

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Allow for different _folder note_ naming scheme when reading sorting specs from _folder notes_

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
- New feature for advanced users: support for **regular expressions** in specifying the **target folder(s)** for custom sorting
- New feature: support for using a **folder name** only to specify the **target folder(s)** for custom sorting
- **update** of relevant **documentation**: [Options for target-folder: matching](https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort/blob/master/docs/manual.md#options-for-target-folder-matching)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
- new feature: support for matching of _starred_ items
  - allows easy pushing of starred items to the top in File Explorer (a simplest usage scenario) 
- more user friendly handling of the condition when the File Explorer is not available (disabled)
  - new dedicated icon plus detailed error message in the console

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
New feature: support for simple explicit regexp-alike expressions.
The `\d` or `\[0-9]` matches a single digit

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
A couple of minor and advanced enhancements:
- more human-friendly: accept both `sortspec` and `sortspec.md` notes in scanning for custom sorting specifications
- advanced feature: ability to assign evaluation priorities to sorting groups (new operators: `/!` `/!!` `/!!!`)
- advanced feature: ability to combine sorting groups (new `/+` operator introduced)
   - documentation pending for this feature

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Major new feature added: ability to group and **sort by notes and folders metadata** fields. This opens a whole new area of possibilities of arranging items in File Explorer.

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Added suport for intentional pure alphabetical sorting, which is different from the default A-Z representing the natural ordering.

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Fixed a minor bug of ribbon icon state not reflecting the actual state of the plugin in some scenarios

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Improved handling of the plugin setting for designated note containing sorting specification (made it less confusing and more human friendly)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Reaching the official community plugins list at https://obsidian.md/plugins deserves a dedicated release :-)

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Enabled one-liner custom sorting specifications

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Introduced `normalizePath(...)` when consuming a path to note in plugin settings

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Code cleanup resulting from Obsidian team review of pull request to Community Plugins list

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Bugfix release - #17 Plugin closes most recent tab

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
A major new feature added: modification and creation time for folders is derived from their files (immediate children) to support sorting by modification and creation date also for folders. New explicitly named sorting types introduced for that purpose, e.g. `advanced modified` on top of the preexising `modified`

#13 Sort by modification date, treating folder and files equally

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Exposed a new plugin setting to enable / disable notifications

#14: Disable notification options

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
No code changes, no functional changes
Release created after adding the GNU LGPL v3 in order to submit the plugin to obsidian.md community plugins

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
- bugfix for the setting of additional note to read sorting specification from. It is no longer ignored - so far the default value of 'Inbox/Inbox.md' was always used regardless of what the user configured in plugin settings
- updated the build configuration and dependencies from the most recent official Obsidian plugin guidelines in obsidianmd/obsidian-sample-plugin. This also imposed some code updates to make it more verbose and strict about typing

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Two new enhancements, as requested via issues:

https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort/issues/3: Feature Request: Disable Status bar entry
https://github.com/SebastianMC/obsidian-custom-sort/issues/4: Feature Request: Activate / Deactivate Custom Sort through command

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: Bugfix for simplistic sorting configurations, when the outsiders matching group was specified for files or for folders only.

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description:
Added new feature: imposing inheritance of sorting rules per child and descendant folders (as per #1)
Tested against pre-release version of Obsidian 0.16.1

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: More user-friendly error message when no custom sorting specification was found at all

releases.author: SebastianMC
releases.description: First public release of the Obsidian custom sort plugin
